validations.command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/round285-review-cargo-target cabal test mlf2-test --test-options='--match "charIsAsciiPunctuation classifies ASCII punctuation Char values through native execution"'
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/round285-review-cargo-target cabal test mlf2-test --test-options='--match "classifies native-lowerable primitive support from the shared primitive inventory owner"'

evidence_summary: Narrowed review of the integrated round-285 worktree found the implementation in scope: public Prelude charIsAsciiPunctuation : Char -> Bool is backed by __char_is_ascii_punctuation; runtime and LLVM/native lowering classify exactly ASCII punctuation ranges 0x21..0x2f, 0x3a..0x40, 0x5b..0x60, and 0x7b..0x7e as true and reject letters, digits, space, and non-ASCII lambda. The focused native punctuation matcher, primitive inventory matcher, and git diff --check passed. Docs/changelog stay within ASCII punctuation scope and do not claim Unicode punctuation, locale, regex, formatting, parser parity, String/List Char conversion, platform/proof, roadmap status, or controller-state expansion.

completion_pointers.text: round-285 completed item-285-char-is-ascii-punctuation-native-tracer: charIsAsciiPunctuation : Char -> Bool classifies ASCII punctuation ranges 0x21..0x2f, 0x3a..0x40, 0x5b..0x60, and 0x7b..0x7e as true and letters, digits, space, and non-ASCII lambda as false through source checking, run-program, backend LLVM/object validation, emit-native/native object validation, and linked native execution; reviewer evidence: git diff --check, focused native punctuation matcher, and primitive inventory matcher passed.
